title: Video 12 MCP Token Stack

audio.note: Each segment uses the same HeyGen source with mediaStart equal to segment timing.start.

renderRules: Audio is master; visual events are timed relative to each segment start.
Use visualEvents[].at when available; resolve visualEvents[].keyword from transcript timing when provided.
Use assets.broll only when status exists or user has captured the required file; otherwise render with component mock fallback.
Real source screenshots and recordings should replace generic mock content when evidence matters.

requiredAssets.captureBrief: Browser open to Cloudflare 'Code Mode' blog/article. Slow scroll past hero. Hover/highlight the search() + execute() phrasing. Linger on the 1,000 input tokens claim or equivalent metric. No audio.
Browser open to the Anthropic 'code execution + MCP' post. Show the page header and the Google Drive -> attach to Salesforce example paragraph. Cursor underlines that sentence once. No audio.
Anthropic docs page covering programmatic tool calling. Scroll to the line stating MCP connector tools cannot be called programmatically. Cursor drags across that sentence. Hold for emphasis.

Finder OR terminal `tree` of skills/video-production showing 02-heygen-vo/, 09-stills/, 03-remotion/ paths. Cursor opens one file (e.g. video-production SKILL.md) briefly to show real procedure content.

Split-terminal recording: left runs a CLI command (e.g. jarvis-price stage QQQ) finishing fast; right runs same task via an MCP-style tool with schema load visible. HyperFrames mock is acceptable substitute.
Short inset (~5-8s) of `/mcp` command running in Claude Code terminal, showing connected servers list. Used as terminal inset only, not full screen.

Five static screenshots (not a video clip): (1) Cloudflare Code Mode page, (2) Anthropic code-execution post, (3) Arcade benchmark page, (4) Scalekit MCP article, (5) one JARVIS test-bench screenshot. Save as 5 PNGs in this directory.

props.text_secondary: MCP, Tool Search, Skills, CLI, Code Mode

props.text: Which layer should do which job?

segments.narrationPurpose: Old failure mode: tool schemas flood context before useful work begins
Tool Search improvement: search-driven expansion, not upfront schemas

labels: After: search, then load only what matters

segments.narrationPurpose: Prevent oversimplification: three caveats about Tool Search

props.text: It does not shrink every result.
It does not make retrieval perfect.

props.center_label: MCP is improved, not magically solved.

segments.narrationPurpose: Reliability tradeoff made concrete with benchmark numbers
Direct address: layer thesis

props.text: Do not make one layer do every job.

segments.narrationPurpose: Cloudflare Code Mode source proof
Kinetic number collapse: 1.17M -> 1K tokens via discover/inspect/execute

props.text: Context should carry reasoning, not every instruction manual.

segments.narrationPurpose: Data movement waste: model is transporting, not reasoning

props.text: Keep intermediate data out of context.

segments.narrationPurpose: Caveat: MCP connector tools cannot be called programmatically
Senior-engineer security warning around sandboxed code execution
Scoped MCP: load less on purpose with group selector and config

props.text: Scoped MCP: load less on purpose.

segments.narrationPurpose: Skills are procedure, not live access
JARVIS uses skills to keep domain rules out of unrelated work

segments.narrationPurpose: Output filtering: messy payload cleaned to three fields
TOON: useful for flat rows, careful with nested data
Full architecture comparison table

props.text: Do not make one tool carry every job.

segments.narrationPurpose: JARVIS layered operating architecture
Convert argument into measurable test plan
Migration phase 1: immediate free wins
Migration phase 2: procedures to skills, deterministic to scripts, filter outputs
Migration phase 3: heavy patterns + sandboxing warning
Correct three common wrong takes via flip cards

props.green: Tool Search helps but has caveats.
